text.title: Serverless Cost Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the serverless-cost-optimization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Memory MUST be tuned to the smallest setting that meets duration + cold-start SLO. Lambda pricing is per-GB-second; over-provisioned memory is the single biggest waste source.

rationale.source: AWS Lambda Power Tuning; Faion FinOps audits.

rule.rationale: Lambda memory also scales CPU; over-sizing pays for unused CPU. Power Tuning often finds 30-60% saving with no latency change.

rule.statement: Provisioned concurrency MUST be removed for functions with > 50% idle PC over 14 days. Auto-scaling PC MUST replace static PC where traffic is predictable but variable.

rationale.source: AWS Application Auto Scaling docs; PC cost postmortems.

rule.rationale: Idle PC pays for capacity that does not absorb cold starts; auto-scaling matches PC to actual demand.

rule.statement: Per-invocation downstream calls (DynamoDB GetItem, S3 PUT, SQS SendMessage) MUST be batched (BatchGetItem, S3 Multipart, SendMessageBatch) when batches > 1 are achievable.
rule.rationale: Per-request pricing on downstream services often dominates Lambda compute cost; batching can cut downstream cost 50-80%.

rule.statement: Event source mappings (SQS, Kinesis) MUST be configured with the largest practical batch size and reasonable batch window. Default batch-size=1 multiplies cost.
rule.rationale: Event source defaults rarely match production needs; tuning batch settings is a 10× cost lever.

rule.statement: Cost report MUST compute break-even RPS vs containers (Fargate / Cloud Run). Above this RPS, container migration MUST be evaluated.

rationale.source: Serverless vs containers cost case studies.

rule.rationale: Pay-per-invocation has a crossover RPS where reserved compute wins; failing to recompute as RPS grows leaves money on the table.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (billing, metrics, downstream cost, PC data), apply r1 → r5 and publish the report.
rule.rationale: Order encodes ROI: memory first, PC idle second, batching third, event source fourth, break-even last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) spend < $100/mo, (b) container migration already planned, (c) SLO at risk. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Cost ceremony in these contexts wastes engineering capacity.
